realise the importance and the uses of a claim form, as well as outline the main sections contained in it

paragraphs.text: In the previous chapter, we have explained that a general insurance policy is an important contract governing the obligations of the insurer and the rights of the insured. In this chapter, we will examine the insurance policy in detail. Besides the policy document, there are other important insurance documents that will be explained. We shall begin with an insurance document that initiates the application for most insurance contracts – the proposal form.
paragraphs.textJP: 前章では、損害保険証券が保険者の義務と被保険者の権利を規律する重要な契約であることを説明しました。本章では、保険証券を詳細に検討します。保険証券のほかにも、保険契約の成立または管理のために使用される重要な保険書類があります。以下はその概要です。

paragraphs.text: The proposal form (application form) is the most common tool by which the insurer receives information about the risks to be insured. The information provided by the proposer (intending insured) in most cases forms the only basis upon which the underwriter decides whether or not to accept the risk and on what terms and premium rates. As such, it is a very important document, as you will see in the next section. See Appendix 6A and Appendix 6B for a sample copy of proposal forms for Private Motor Insurance and Public Liability Insurance respectively.
paragraphs.textJP: 申込書は、保険者が付保されるリスクに関する情報を受け取る最も一般的な手段です。ほとんどの場合、申込者（保険加入予定者）が提供する情報は、引受人がリスクを引き受けるかどうか、引き受ける場合はどのような料率、条件で引き受けるかを決定する唯一の根拠となります。

paragraphs.text: The proposal form is the basis of the insurance contract and is incorporated into the contract. This has the effect of making the proposal form part of the contract, even though it is not actually reproduced and printed with the policy document. Therefore, the proposer has to be particularly careful to supply correct, accurate and comprehensive information when completing the proposal form, as it will become part of the contract.
paragraphs.textJP: 申込書は保険契約の基礎であり、契約に組み込まれます。これにより、申込書は保険証券に実際に複製・印刷されていなくても、契約の一部となる効果があります。したがって、申込者は申込書の質問に特に注意して回答しなければなりません。

paragraphs.text: The fact that the proposal form is the basis of the contract is further strengthened by the declaration of the proposer at the end of the proposal form. The declaration is also considered as part of the contract. By signing the declaration, the proposer is confirming that the information which has been provided to the insurer is true to the best of his knowledge and belief. Details of the declaration will be discussed later in this chapter.
paragraphs.textJP: 申込書が契約の基礎であるという事実は、申込書の末尾にある申込者の宣言によってさらに強化されます。宣言も契約の一部とみなされます。宣言に署名することにより、申込者は提供された情報が真実かつ正確であることを確認しています。

paragraphs.text: Let us now proceed to look at the questions asked in various types of proposal forms. Proposal Form
paragraphs.textJP: では、各種申込書で尋ねられる質問について見ていきましょう。

subsections.label: B
subsections.title: Questions Asked In A Proposal Form
subsections.titleJP: 申込書で尋ねられる質問

paragraphs.text: Proposal forms are of variable lengths depending on the nature of the risk, and the information which an insurer will need to be able to underwrite the risk.
paragraphs.textJP: 申込書の長さは、リスクの性質および保険者がリスクを引き受けるために必要とする情報によって異なります。

paragraphs.text:
Basically, questions asked in a proposal form can be broadly classified into five main sections:
▪ Details Of The Proposer And/Or The Insured
▪ Period Of Insurance These questions are not specific to the actual risk itself.
▪ Details Of The Risk To Be Insured
▪ The Subject Matter Of Insurance
▪ The Insurance History Of The Proposer And/Or The Insured These questions are risk specific in the sense that they relate to the form of risk for which cover is being sought. These are the questions that will assist the underwriter in determining whether or not to accept the risk, what terms to impose, and what price (premium) to charge.
paragraphs.textJP:
基本的に、申込書で尋ねられる質問は大きく5つのセクションに分類されます。
▪ 申込者および／または被保険者の詳細
▪ 保険期間（これらの質問は実際のリスク自体に固有のものではありません）
▪ 付保されるリスクの詳細
▪ 保険の対象物
▪ 申込者および／または被保険者の保険歴

subsections.label: B1
subsections.title: Details Of The Proposer And/Or The Insured
subsections.titleJP: 申込者および／または被保険者の詳細

paragraphs.text: In general insurance, the proposer is usually insuring themselves or property which they own. However, sometimes, the proposer can be another person who has an insurable interest in the subject matter of the insurance, e.g. the wife on the life of her husband in a Personal Accident Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 損害保険では、申込者は通常、自己または自己が所有する財物を付保しています。ただし、申込者が保険の対象物に対して被保険利益を有する別の者である場合もあります（例：傷害保険における夫の生命に対する妻）。

paragraphs.text: The details required of the proposer and/or the insured are: (a) Name in full - This is required for the purpose of identification. (b) Address - This is required for identification and communication. It can also be a material factor (risk location) for the underwriting of the risk, e.g. in the case of Fire or Theft Insurance. (c) Occupation or type of business - This provides information for the insurer to underwrite the risk, especially with Personal Accident, Motor, Theft and Fire Insurance policies. It is obvious that the type of business can have a material bearing on the hazards involved in the risk. (d) Interested party - This will enable the insurer to know if there is any other party interested in the policy in the event of a claim, e.g. a mortgagee, or an owner named under a hire-purchase agreement. (e) Other questions - Depending on the class of insurance, various questions may be asked on the gender, nationality, date of birth, demerit points (in Motor Insurance), etc.
paragraphs.textJP:
申込者および／または被保険者に求められる詳細は以下のとおりです。
(a) 氏名 — 身元確認のために必要です。
(b) 住所 — 身元確認と連絡のために必要です。リスクの引受にとっての重要な要素（リスクの所在地）にもなりえます。

paragraphs.text: In general insurance business, the period concerned is normally a full year (12 months) from a date as determined by the proposer, although exceptions may arise, e.g. Travel Insurance where a shorter period may be indicated for coverage of a single trip, or Foreign Domestic Worker (Maid) Insurance, where the period of insurance is usually 14 or 26 months. However, for most classes of insurance, the premium rates applied assume a normal policy period of one year.
paragraphs.textJP: 損害保険事業では、対象期間は通常、申込者が決定する日付から満1年（12ヶ月）ですが、例外が生じることもあります。例えば、旅行保険では1回の旅行のカバーとしてより短い期間が指定される場合や、外国人家事労働者保険では就労ビザの残存期間に合わせた期間が設定される場合があります。

paragraphs.text: The policy period begins on the policy’s effective date (the time and date that the coverage under the policy goes into effect) and it ends on the expiry date (the time and date that the coverage under the policy expires). When Coverage Is In Effect
paragraphs.textJP: 保険期間は、保険証券の発効日（保険証券に基づくカバーが発効する日時）に始まり、満了日（保険証券に基づくカバーが満了する日時）に終了します。

paragraphs.text: In most cases, the policy will lapse on the expiry date, unless the insured gives instructions to renew the policy, and the insurer accepts the renewal instructions.
paragraphs.textJP: ほとんどの場合、被保険者が保険証券の更新を指示し、保険者がその更新指示を受け入れない限り、保険証券は満了日に失効します。

subsections.label: B3
subsections.title: Details Of The Risk To Be Insured
subsections.titleJP: 付保されるリスクの詳細

paragraphs.text: In this section, the insurer attempts to elicit sufficient information to underwrite the risk. This process will involve a decision as to the insurability of the risk and determining the premium charge and other contract terms.
paragraphs.textJP: このセクションでは、保険者はリスクを引き受けるのに十分な情報を引き出そうとします。このプロセスには、リスクの付保適格性の判断、保険料の決定、およびその他の契約条件の決定が含まれます。

paragraphs.text: Questions asked here vary widely between classes of insurance.
paragraphs.textJP: ここで尋ねられる質問は、保険の種類によって大きく異なります。

例は以下のとおりです。
(a) 火災保険：リスクの建築構造はどのようなものか。建物が木造であれば、コンクリート造の建物よりもほぼ確実にリスクが高くなります。リスクの所在地はどこか。これは消防署からの距離を判断するために重要です。
(b) 自動車保険：車両の用途は何か。自家用か商用か。ドライバーの年齢は。事故歴はあるか。

paragraphs.text: The subject matter of insurance is the object exposed to risk, such as people, building, house, factory, stock, vehicle, aircraft or sea vessel. The subject matter must be capable of being identified.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険の対象物とは、リスクにさらされている物体です。例えば、人、建物、住宅、工場、在庫、車両、航空機、船舶などです。対象物は識別可能でなければなりません。

paragraphs.text: Proposal forms thus make provisions to record the description and the location, etc. of the insured subject matter. The proposer must take care to provide accurate descriptions of the subject matter. For example, in Fire Insurance policy forms, there is a condition which states that any mis-description of the insured subject matter or omission of material facts will not render the insurer liable for the policy, which means no claim will be paid.
paragraphs.textJP: したがって、申込書は対象物の説明、所在地等を記録するための項目を設けています。申込者は対象物の正確な記述を提供するよう注意しなければなりません。例えば、火災保険の申込書には、対象物の記述の誤りにより保険者はいかなる責任も負わない旨の条件が記載されています。

subsections.label: B5
subsections.title: The Insurance History Of The Proposer And/Or The Insured
subsections.titleJP: 申込者および／または被保険者の保険歴

paragraphs.text: In addition to obtaining information on the past claim history of the insured, this section also attempts to solicit information on whether the proposer is currently and/or has previously insured with other insurers.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者の過去の保険金請求履歴に関する情報を取得することに加えて、このセクションでは、申込者が現在および／または過去に他の保険者で付保しているかどうかについての情報も求めます。

paragraphs.text: Thus, questions will be asked on whether the proposer has ever been declined, cancelled, accepted on special terms, or refused renewal by another insurer. If so, full details as to the name of the insurer, policy number, type of cover, etc. are required.
paragraphs.textJP: したがって、申込者が他の保険者から加入を断られたか、解約されたか、特別条件で引き受けられたか、更新を拒否されたことがあるかどうかの質問がなされます。ある場合は、保険者名、保険証券番号、カバーの種類等の完全な詳細が必要です。

paragraphs.text: These questions assist the underwriter to be put on guard against proposers with a poor claims experience, or those intending to defraud insurers.
paragraphs.textJP: これらの質問は、引受人が保険金請求実績の悪い申込者や、保険者を欺こうとする申込者に対して警戒するのに役立ちます。

すべての質問の後、申込者は以下を行うことが求められます。
▪ 回答の真実性および完全性を宣言する（時に保証する）
▪ すべての重要事実を告知したことを述べる
▪ 申込書が契約の基礎であることに同意する
▪ 通常の条件、規定、および保険証券の条件を受け入れることに同意する

paragraphs.text: NOTE: While the agent can assist the proposer to complete the proposal form, the latter must read through and check the answers and sign the form himself. The agent must not sign the form on the proposer’s behalf.
paragraphs.textJP: 注意：代理店は申込者が申込書を記入するのを手伝うことができますが、申込者自身が回答を読み通して確認し、自ら署名しなければなりません。代理店が申込者に代わって署名してはなりません。

paragraphs.text: As mentioned earlier, the purpose of the declaration is for the proposer to confirm that the information provided is true to the best of his knowledge and belief.
paragraphs.textJP: 前述のとおり、宣言の目的は、申込者が提供した情報が自己の知識と信念に基づき真実であることを確認することです。

paragraphs.text: Besides including a declaration, every proposal form contains a warning statement in relation to Section 23(5) of the Insurance Act 1966, which states as follows: “You are to disclose in this proposal form, fully and faithfully, all the facts which you know or ought to know; otherwise you may receive nothing from the policy.”
paragraphs.textJP: 宣言に加えて、すべての申込書にはInsurance Act 1966第23条(5)に関連する警告文が含まれており、以下のように述べています。「本申込書において、あなたが知っている、または知るべきすべての事実を完全かつ誠実に告知してください。さもなければ、保険証券に基づく給付を一切受け取れない場合があります。」

paragraphs.text: The purpose of the warning statement is, therefore, to caution the proposer about the facts to be disclosed and the danger if any material facts are not dutifully disclosed. It also signifies the duty of disclosure by the proposer (intending insured), thereby reinforcing the principle of utmost good faith. Compare & Contrast Readers can gain a more complete knowledge of proposal forms, by comparing and contrasting the proposal forms of different insurers, concerning the questions asked for the same class of insurance and the declarations to be made. &
paragraphs.textJP: したがって、警告文の目的は、告知されるべき事実および重要事実が適切に告知されない場合の危険性について申込者に注意を促すことです。また、申込者（保険加入予定者）の告知義務を示すものであり、最大善意の原則を補強するものです。

paragraphs.text: It is not always possible to issue an insurance policy as soon as cover is required by an insured. Thus, while negotiations are underway, or while further information is being sought (e.g. risk surveys), temporary cover is given, and a Cover Note is issued. A Cover Note may also be useful, when the insured has to prove to some other party of the existence of insurance or that insurance has been effected. The format of the Cover Note will vary with different classes of insurance.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者がカバーを必要とした直後に保険証券を発行できるとは限りません。したがって、交渉が進行中の間、または追加情報を求めている間（例：リスク調査）、一時的なカバーが付与され、カバーノートが発行されます。カバーノートは、要求されているカバーが複雑で、その条件の詰めに時間がかかる場合にも有用です。

paragraphs.text:
The Cover Note usually contains a wording to the effect that it is:
▪ valid only for a certain period;
▪ subject to the usual terms, exclusions and conditions of the insurance policy for that class of business; and
▪ subject to any further special clauses, if applicable, and specified in the Cover Note.
paragraphs.textJP:
カバーノートには通常、以下の趣旨の文言が含まれています。
▪ 一定の期間のみ有効であること
▪ 当該事業種類の保険証券の通常の条件、免責事項、および条件に従うこと
▪ 該当する場合、カバーノートに記載された追加の特別条項に従うこと

カバーノートは一時的な保険証券と考えることができ、通常、付保されるリスクを特定するための十分な詳細を含みます。例えば：
▪ 被保険者の氏名と住所
▪ 保険金額
▪ 保険期間（通常は短期間、15日または30日程度）
▪ カバーされるリスク
▪ 対象物の記述

paragraphs.text: Soon after the Cover Note has been given to the insured, a formal policy of insurance will be issued, and it is cross-referenced to the Cover Note number.
paragraphs.textJP: カバーノートが被保険者に交付された後間もなく、正式な保険証券が発行され、カバーノート番号に相互参照されます。

paragraphs.text: A Cover Note has the same legal status as the actual insurance policy. If a loss occurs during the period of insurance, when a Cover Note is in force (i.e. before a formal policy of insurance is issued), the insurer is liable to pay the claim, subject to the standard policy wording, unless special terms have been included in the Cover Note.
paragraphs.textJP: カバーノートは実際の保険証券と同じ法的効力を有します。カバーノートが有効な保険期間中（すなわち正式な保険証券が発行される前）に損失が発生した場合、特別な条件が付されていない限り、保険者は標準的な保険証券の文言に従い保険金を支払う義務を負います。

paragraphs.text: When insurance is compulsory by law, the law also requires that a prescribed Certificate of Insurance is issued to prove that a policy is in force. This does make sense. For instance, in the case of a motor accident, how will the police or someone involved know that a driver has complied with the law and has a valid insurance policy? We can make the driver carry the actual policy document with him all the time, but this is cumbersome. A small Certificate of Insurance thus becomes useful in such a situation.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険が法律で義務付けられている場合、法律は保険証券が有効であることを証明する所定の保険証明書の発行も義務付けています。これは合理的です。例えば、自動車事故の場合、警察や関係者がドライバーが法律を遵守していることをどのように知ることができるでしょうか。

paragraphs.text: A Certificate of Insurance is only evidence of, and is not equivalent to, a policy of insurance.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証明書は、保険証券の証拠にすぎず、保険証券と同等のものではありません。

paragraphs.text: Certificates of Insurance are normally used in Marine Cargo, Motor and Work Injury Compensation Insurance (sometimes for Card Protection and Travel Insurance, as well as Group Insurance to individual members to evidence their participation in a group plan).
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証明書は通常、海上貨物保険、自動車保険、労災補償保険で使用されます（カード保護保険、旅行保険、および団体保険で個人メンバーの団体プランへの参加を証明するためにも使用されることがあります）。

paragraphs.text: In Singapore, according to the Motor Vehicles (Third-Party Risks and Compensation) Act 1960, it is an offence to drive or use a motor vehicle on a public road, without a valid insurance policy insuring for at least “Act” cover, i.e. death of or bodily injury to third party.
paragraphs.textJP: シンガポールでは、Motor Vehicles (Third-Party Risks and Compensation) Act 1960（自動車法1960年）に基づき、少なくとも「Act」カバー（第三者の死亡または身体傷害）を付保する有効な保険証券なしに公道で自動車を運転または使用することは犯罪です。

paragraphs.text: In addition to the insurance policy, a Certificate of Insurance in the prescribed form required by the Act is issued to provide evidence of insurance to the police (as and when required), and the road tax collection centre authorised by the Land Transport Authority of Singapore. See Appendix 6C for a sample of the Private Motor Car Certificate of Insurance.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券に加えて、同法が定める所定の様式の保険証明書が、警察（必要に応じて）およびシンガポール陸上交通庁が認可した自動車税徴収センターに保険の証拠を提供するために発行されます。サンプルはAppendix 6Cを参照してください。

paragraphs.text: This Certificate of Insurance must be kept by the motorist at all times. In the event of an accident, details of the respective parties can be exchanged.
paragraphs.textJP: この保険証明書は、ドライバーが常に携帯しなければなりません。事故の場合、それぞれの当事者の詳細を交換することができます。

paragraphs.text: When the motor vehicle is sold, the law requires that the motor vehicle must be registered in the new owner’s name within seven days of the sale. The insurer requires the Certificate of Insurance to be returned immediately after the sale (i.e. it is not transferable, unless the consent of the insurer is obtained. The latter will then issue a fresh Certificate of Insurance in the name of the new owner).
paragraphs.textJP: 自動車が売却された場合、法律は自動車が売却から7日以内に新しい所有者の名義で登録されることを義務付けています。保険者は、保険証明書を売却後直ちに返還することを求めます（すなわち、保険者の同意がない限り譲渡不可能です）。

paragraphs.text: Different prescribed certificates are issued for commercial vehicles and motorcycles in Singapore.
paragraphs.textJP: シンガポールでは、商用車両と二輪車に対して異なる所定の証明書が発行されます。

paragraphs.text: Under the Work Injury Compensation (Insurance) Regulations 2020, it is required that a copy of the Certificate of Insurance must be prominently displayed at the insured’s place of business. Where the insured has more than one place of business, the Certificate of Insurance must be displayed at each place of business at which he employs any employee covered by the insurance. As the insurer provides only one certificate, it is the responsibility of the insured to make sufficient copies for display. See Appendix 6D for a sample Work Injury Compensation Insurance Certificate.
paragraphs.textJP: Work Injury Compensation (Insurance) Regulations 2020に基づき、保険証明書のコピーが被保険者の事業所に目立つように掲示されなければなりません。被保険者が複数の事業所を有する場合、保険証明書は各事業所に掲示されなければなりません。

paragraphs.text: The Motor Insurance and Work Injury Compensation Certificates of Insurance provide formal proof of the existence of insurance cover required by law. However, in Marine Cargo Insurance, the certificate has a different function. It is not required by law.
paragraphs.textJP: 自動車保険および労災補償保険の保険証明書は、法律が求める保険カバーの存在の正式な証明を提供します。しかし、海上貨物保険では、証明書は異なる機能を持ちます。法律で義務付けられているものではありません。

paragraphs.text: Often, insureds have regular shipments of cargo. It is sometimes inconvenient for the insurer to issue an insurance policy for each and every shipment.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者は定期的な貨物の積み出しを行うことがしばしばあります。保険者が積み出しごとに保険証券を発行することは不便な場合があります。

paragraphs.text: Thus, an Open Cover - as a sort of master policy - is issued. This is the most common form of policy for insuring cargo. It incorporates pre-agreed details of the class of the vessels, voyage, types of cargoes, basis of valuation and limits, terms and conditions of coverage, together with a rating structure. Certificates are then issued for each shipment and/or each buyer. The insured has to make declarations (monthly or quarterly) concerning all shipments, so that the appropriate premiums can be charged.
paragraphs.textJP: したがって、一種のマスターポリシーとしてOpen Cover（包括予定保険）が発行されます。これは貨物を付保するための最も一般的な保険証券の形態です。Open Coverには、船舶の等級、航路、貨物の種類、評価基準と限度額、カバーの条件、および船積みごとに適用される料率スケジュールの事前合意された詳細が組み込まれています。

paragraphs.text: An insurance policy is not the contract, but it is the written evidence of the insurance contract. This contact is usually in force before the policy is issued.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券は契約そのものではありませんが、保険契約の書面による証拠です。この契約は通常、保険証券が発行される前に効力を生じています。

paragraphs.text: As policies are legal documents, their wording is very important. In almost every case, the document is prepared by the insurer, and it sets out the terms of the insurance contract.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券は法的文書であるため、その文言は非常に重要です。ほぼすべての場合、文書は保険者によって作成され、保険契約の条件を定めています。

paragraphs.text: Since the policy is usually prepared by the insurer, any ambiguity in its wording will be construed against the insurer, in favour of the insured. This is the application of the “contra proferentem” rule, which holds that the person drawing up a legal document is responsible for any defects in it and cannot profit from such defects.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券は通常保険者によって作成されるため、その文言のいかなる曖昧さも、被保険者に有利に保険者に不利に解釈されます。これは「作成者不利の原則」（contra proferentem）の適用であり、法的文書を作成した者がその欠陥に責任を負うとするものです。

paragraphs.text: In Singapore, policy wordings for Fire, Work Injury Compensation, Motor and Marine Insurance do not vary a lot among insurers. However, different insurers use different sizes, typography or colours for their policy documents.
paragraphs.textJP: シンガポールでは、火災保険、労災補償保険、自動車保険、海上保険の保険証券の文言は、保険者間でそれほど大きく異なりません。ただし、保険者によって保険証券の書類のサイズ、書体、色は異なります。

paragraphs.text: As is the trend in the United States and the United Kingdom, policy wordings have been redrafted using layman terms (plain English) to help the man in the street to understand the contract better. In Singapore, some insurers are issuing plain English policies, mostly for, personal lines, such as Foreign Domestic Worker (Maid), Golfer’s, Health, Home, Motor, Personal Accident and Travel Insurance.
paragraphs.textJP: 米国や英国の傾向と同様に、一般の人が契約をより理解しやすくするために、保険証券の文言は平易な用語（プレーンイングリッシュ）で再起草されています。シンガポールでは、一部の保険者が、主に外国人家事労働者保険や旅行保険などの個人分野でプレーンイングリッシュの保険証券を発行しています。

paragraphs.text: More insurance companies are now issuing what is termed as a scheduled policy, i.e. all the normal sections in a policy are pre-printed, and any amendments and specific details are stated in the Schedule (see Appendix 6E for sample policy).
paragraphs.textJP: 現在、より多くの保険会社が、スケジュールド・ポリシーと呼ばれるものを発行しています。すなわち、保険証券のすべての標準的なセクションが事前に印刷されており、修正や特定の詳細事項はスケジュール（別表）に記載されます。サンプルはAppendix 6Eを参照してください。

paragraphs.text: A scheduled policy form is usually divided into the following sections:
paragraphs.textJP: スケジュールド・ポリシーの書式は、通常、以下のセクションに分かれています。

paragraphs.text: It gives the name and address of the insurer and other relevant information which varies from company to company.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者の名称と住所、および会社によって異なるその他の関連情報を記載します。

paragraphs.text: This clause recites or states the parties to the contract (not mentioning their names). It also makes reference to the proposal form as the basis of the contract, and the premium, as having been paid or agreed to be paid by the insured (policyholder), as a consideration for the insurance provided by the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: この条項は、契約の当事者（名前は記載せず）を叙述または記載します。また、申込書を契約の基礎として参照し、保険者による保険の提供の約因として、被保険者（保険契約者）によって保険料が支払われたか支払われることに同意されたことに言及します。

subsections.label: A3
subsections.title: Operative Or Insuring Clause
subsections.titleJP: 補償条項（保険引受条項）

paragraphs.text: The clause sets out, in detail, precisely what the policy is insuring. Sometimes, policies will be on a “named perils” (or “specified perils”) basis, which means that the causes of insured loss are stated individually. WE INSURE FOR LOSS CAUSED BY: ◼ __________ ◼ __________ ◼ __________ ◼ __________ ◼ __________ ◼ __________ ◼ __________ WE INSURE AGAINST ALL RISKS OF LOSS, EXCEPT FOR: ◼ _________ ◼ _________ ◼ _________ ◼ _________ ◼ _________ ◼ _________ ◼ _________ Named Perils Versus All Risks Named Perils All risks
paragraphs.textJP: この条項は、保険証券が何を付保しているかを詳細に正確に定めます。保険証券は「記名危険事故」（「特定危険事故」）ベースである場合があり、これは被保険の損失の原因が個別に記載されていることを意味します。

paragraphs.text: If the policy covers “all risks”, then all forms of unforeseen loss and sudden damage are covered, subject to specific policy exclusions.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券が「オールリスク」をカバーする場合、特定の保険証券の免責事項を条件として、すべての形態の予見不可能な損失および突然の損害がカバーされます。

補償条項は非常に短い場合がありますが、自動車保険の場合は例えば以下のような複数のセクションを持ちます。
▪ セクションI — 自動車に対する保険
▪ セクションII — 第三者に対する賠償責任
▪ セクションIII — 医療費給付
▪ セクションIV — 傷害給付

paragraphs.text: It will be the sole responsibility of the insured to establish that a loss falls within the Operative Clause in the event of a claim arising under a policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券に基づく保険金請求が発生した場合、損失が補償条項に該当することを立証するのは被保険者の単独の責任です。
このセクションは、保険証券を保険契約者に個人的なものとし、当該保険証券に関するすべての情報を含みます。当該事業種類のすべての保険証券に共通する標準情報は、他のセクションに含まれます。スケジュールは事業種類によって異なります。

paragraphs.text: Exclusions or exceptions are perils, risks, subject matter and other circumstances that are specifically stated as not covered in an insurance policy. They define the boundaries of policy cover and describe situations in which the insurer will not pay or admit liability for claims. Exclusions help to keep insurance premiums more uniform and reasonable.
paragraphs.textJP: 免責事項または除外事項とは、保険証券にカバーされないものとして具体的に記載された危険事故、リスク、対象物、およびその他の状況です。保険カバーの境界を定義し、保険者が保険金を支払わないか賠償責任を認めない状況を記述します。免責事項は保険カバーの範囲を明確にするのに役立ちます。

paragraphs.text: Exclusions in a policy vary, depending on the type of coverage and the situations which the contract is designed to cover. Every insurance contract has exclusions. Even specified peril insurance policies, which automatically exclude any perils not specifically stated, list certain exclusions separately to explain or emphasise perils excluded from coverage.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券の免責事項は、カバーの種類および契約がカバーするよう設計されている状況によって異なります。すべての保険契約には免責事項があります。具体的に記載されていない危険事故を自動的に除外する特定危険事故保険でも、一定の免責事項が別途記載されています。

paragraphs.text: As for a packaged or multi-sectioned policy, each section of the policy will contain its own specific exclusions. In addition, there is another section entitled “General Exceptions” which contains exclusions applying to all sections of the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: パッケージ型または複数セクション型の保険証券の場合、各セクションにはそれぞれ固有の免責事項が含まれます。加えて、保険証券のすべてのセクションに適用される免責事項を含む「一般的免責事項」というタイトルの別のセクションがあります。

paragraphs.text: It is normally the responsibility of the insurer to prove that a particular exception applies, although the policy wording may require the insured to prove that the exclusion shall not apply.
paragraphs.textJP: 特定の免責事項が適用されることを証明する責任は通常保険者にありますが、保険証券の文言が被保険者に免責事項の不適用を証明することを求める場合もあります。

保険証券に規定される免責事項の目的は以下のとおりです。
▪ モラルハザードの管理を支援する
▪ 特別な引受審査を要するカバーを排除する
▪ 他の保険種類で利用可能なカバーを排除する
▪ 保険に適さないと考えられるリスクに対するカバーを排除する

paragraphs.text: The Conditions section states the “ground rules” for the policy. It describes the responsibilities of both the insurer and the insured.
paragraphs.textJP: 条件のセクションは、保険証券の「基本ルール」を定めます。保険者と被保険者の両方の責任を記述します。

paragraphs.text: The conditions printed in a policy are called express conditions (such as exclusions, warranties, alteration, arbitration, subrogation, claim notification, policy cancellation, etc.) and they regulate the policy contract.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券に印刷された条件は、明示的条件（免責事項、担保条項、変更、仲裁、代位、保険金請求の通知、保険証券の解約等）と呼ばれ、保険契約を規律します。

保険証券には記載されていないが適用される「黙示的条件」もあります。以下が含まれます。
▪ 被保険利益
▪ 最大善意
▪ 対象物の存在
▪ 対象物の同一性
▪ 所有権の証明
▪ 契約の適法性

paragraphs.text: Also, conditions whether expressed or implied can be grouped as: (a) Conditions precedent to the policy - These are conditions which must be fulfilled, before the contract can become valid. For example, the implied condition of utmost good faith, requiring that all material facts must be disclosed during the negotiations leading to the contract. If such conditions are not complied with, there is doubt as to the validity of the entire contract. (b) Conditions subsequent to the policy - These are conditions which must be fulfilled if the contract is to continue, once it becomes binding. For example, under a Personal Accident Insurance policy, the insured is required to inform the insurer of any change in his occupation during the period of insurance. (c) Conditions precedent to liability - These are conditions which must be fulfilled, before the insured is legally entitled to recover a claim under the contract. For example, the insured is required to notify the insurer within a specified notification period, when a loss occurs under his policy. Failure to comply may prejudice the insurer’s decision to pay and the corresponding amount to be paid.
paragraphs.textJP:
また、明示的か黙示的かを問わず、条件は以下のように分類されます。
(a) 保険証券に先行する条件 — 契約が有効になる前に履行されなければならない条件です。例えば、すべての重要事実が保険証券の開始前に告知されなければならないとする、最大善意の黙示的条件です。
(b) 賠償責任に先行する条件 — 保険金が支払われる前に履行されなければならない条件です。例えば、保険金請求の通知に関する条件です。
(c) 契約に後続する条件 — 保険契約が存在するようになった後に適用される条件です。

paragraphs.text: A breach of the implied conditions can affect the validity of the insurance contract. However, a breach of the express conditions may result in the repudiation of the claim, if it is a condition subsequent to the contract and/or precedent to liability, or the entire contract if it is a condition precedent to the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 黙示的条件の違反は保険契約の有効性に影響を与えることがあります。しかし、明示的条件の違反は、それが契約に後続する条件および／または賠償責任に先行する条件である場合は保険金請求の拒否、または保険証券に先行する条件である場合は契約全体の拒否をもたらすことがあります。

paragraphs.text: A warranty is an undertaking by the insured that a certain state of affairs will or will not continue, or that something shall or shall not be done throughout the duration of the insurance contract. It goes to the heart of the contract.
paragraphs.textJP: 担保条項とは、一定の事実の状態が継続する、または継続しない、あるいは保険契約の期間中に一定の事項がなされる、またはなされないという被保険者による約束です。これは契約の核心に関わるものです。

paragraphs.text: A warranty in insurance is not a minor term of the contract, but one of great importance.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険における担保条項は、契約の些末な条件ではなく、非常に重要な条件です。

paragraphs.text: A warranty is essentially a promise made by the insured relating to the facts or to something that the insured agrees to do or not to do. A warranty may relate to past, present or future facts (i.e. it is a promise that something was so or is or will be so), or it may be a continuing warranty, in which the insured promises that a state of affairs will continue to exist. For example, a warranty may require that no work will be carried out at a height greater than 12 metres, or that an intruder alarm is to be kept in good working order and regularly tested.
paragraphs.textJP: 担保条項は、本質的に、事実に関連するか、被保険者が行うことまたは行わないことに同意する事項に関連する被保険者の約束です。担保条項は、過去、現在、または将来の事実に関するもの（すなわち、何かがそうであった、またはそうである、またはそうであるであろうという約束）、または継続的担保条項（被保険者が保険契約期間中ずっと何かを行う、または行わないことを約束するもの）とすることができます。

paragraphs.text: A warranty must be strictly complied with. If it is not, unless there is a waiver by the insurer, cover may terminate, even if the breach did not cause or have any connection with a loss, and even if the breach had been remedied by the time a loss occurred. Termination arises from the date of the breach. Some clauses are worded such that cover (and effectively the contract) will terminate ab initio (from the beginning) in the event of a breach by the insured. In such cases, unless fraud is involved, the premium will have to be refunded to the insured.
paragraphs.textJP: 担保条項は厳格に遵守されなければなりません。遵守されない場合、保険者による免除がない限り、違反が損失を引き起こしたか損失と何らかの関連があるかどうかにかかわらず、また損失発生時までに違反が是正されていたとしても、カバーは終了する可能性があります。終了は違反の日から生じます。

paragraphs.text:
Warranties occur in most classes of insurance and are used by the insurer to make sure that a desirable situation, disclosed as a material fact during the application and the negotiations for the contract of insurance, actually stays desirable and is not allowed to disappear once the insurance contract is formed. Basically, there are two forms of warranties in insurance contracts, namely those which:
▪ simply denote the scope of cover; and
▪ are promissory in nature.
paragraphs.textJP: 担保条項はほとんどの保険種類に存在し、保険契約の申込みおよび交渉の際に重要事実として告知された望ましい状況が、実際に望ましい状態を維持し、保険契約が成立した後に消滅することが許容されないようにするために保険者によって使用されます。

paragraphs.text:
For obvious reasons, they are usually imposed to ensure that:
▪ some form of “good housekeeping” or good management is observed, e.g. in Burglary Insurance, all doors are properly locked, and the intruder alarm system is in good working order; and
▪ features of higher risks are not introduced without the insurer’s knowledge, e.g. in Fire Insurance, no flammable chemicals are stored in the premises.
paragraphs.textJP:
明白な理由から、担保条項は通常、以下を確保するために課されます。
▪ 何らかの「良好な管理」が遵守されること。例えば、盗難保険では、すべてのドアが適切に施錠され、侵入者警報装置が正常に作動していること。
▪ より高いリスクの特徴が保険者の承知なしに導入されないこと。

paragraphs.text: Warranties can also be classified as: (a) Implied Warranties Those that do not appear in the policy but are understood to automatically apply in the contract of insurance, e.g. under Marine Insurance, the vessel insured is seaworthy. (b) Express Warranties Those that are written in the insurance contract, e.g. under Marine Insurance, the goods are packed in tin-lined cases.
paragraphs.textJP:
担保条項は以下のようにも分類されます。
(a) 黙示的担保条項 — 保険証券には記載されていないが、保険契約に自動的に適用されると理解されるもの。例えば、海上保険では、付保された船舶が堪航性を有すること。
(b) 明示的担保条項 — 保険契約に書面で記載されるもの。例えば、保険料支払担保条項。

paragraphs.text: Generally, warranties must be strictly and literally complied with. Noncompliance with a warranty by the insured constitutes a breach of warranty, and the insurer is discharged from liability as from the date of the breach. In other words, the contract is voidable from the date of the breach. However, at times, the insurer may excuse a breach of express warranty if prior notification is received, and an additional premium (if necessary) is paid by the insured.
paragraphs.textJP: 一般に、担保条項は厳格かつ文字通りに遵守されなければなりません。被保険者による担保条項の不遵守は担保条項の違反を構成し、保険者は違反の日から賠償責任を免除されます。言い換えれば、契約は違反の日から取消可能となります。ただし、違反が是正された後に発生した損失について、保険者が賠償責任を有する場合もあります。

paragraphs.text: So, what is the difference between a condition and a warranty? In insurance law, a condition and a warranty have specific meanings.
paragraphs.textJP: では、条件と担保条項の違いは何でしょうか。保険法において、条件と担保条項には特定の意味があります。

paragraphs.text: A warranty can be a condition, but a condition may not be a warranty.
paragraphs.textJP: 担保条項は条件となりえますが、条件は担保条項であるとは限りません。

paragraphs.text: Firstly, let us discuss the meaning of these terms in the context of a noninsurance contract. In such contracts, when a condition has been breached, the party aggrieved can claim for damages. He can even terminate the contact as the contract has been repudiated by the breach. The situation is less serious for a breach of warranty as it is not considered a vital part of the contract i.e., he could claim for damages but not terminate the contract.
paragraphs.textJP: まず、非保険契約の文脈におけるこれらの用語の意味について説明しましょう。そのような契約では、条件が違反された場合、被害を受けた当事者は損害賠償を請求できます。違反により契約が否認されたとして契約を終了させることさえできます。しかし、担保条項が違反された場合、当事者は損害賠償を請求できるに過ぎません。

paragraphs.text: As for insurance contracts, the situation is quite the opposite of that stated above. Warranties play a rather more significant role in insurance law compared to conditions. What is the effect of a breach of warranty in an insurance contract? If the insured has breached a warranty, the insurer is no longer liable as of the date of the breach. In effect, the insurance claims would be invalidated as a result of such a breach of a warranty.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険契約については、上述の状況とはまったく逆です。担保条項は条件と比較して、保険法においてより重要な役割を果たします。保険契約における担保条項の違反の効果は何でしょうか。被保険者が担保条項に違反した場合、保険者は違反の日から賠償責任を免除されます。

paragraphs.text: In most cases, the term “condition” does not relate to the statements of fact or the risk covered within the insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: ほとんどの場合、「条件」という用語は、保険証券内の事実の記載やカバーされるリスクには関連しません。

paragraphs.text: Differentiating warranties and conditions is vital for many reasons. For example, a condition in an insurance policy may state that that the insured is to furnish all material information about himself. If the insured does not do so, it can be argued that the insured is in breach of his policy, but the insurer is unlikely to reject the claim if the insurer can obtain this information elsewhere. The situation is different for warranties as they must be more strictly complied with for the policy to be valid.
paragraphs.textJP: 担保条項と条件を区別することは多くの理由から極めて重要です。例えば、保険証券の条件が、被保険者は自己に関するすべての重要な情報を提供しなければならないと定めている場合。被保険者がそうしない場合、被保険者が保険証券に違反していると主張できますが、違反が担保条項違反として扱われるか、単なる条件違反として扱われるかによって結果が大きく異なります。

paragraphs.text: Refer to the following section of this chapter for a comparison of exclusions/ exceptions, conditions and warranties.
paragraphs.textJP: 免責事項、条件、担保条項の比較については、本章の次のセクションを参照してください。

subsections.label: A8
subsections.title: Comparison Of Exclusions/Exceptions, Conditions & Warranties
subsections.titleJP: 免責事項、条件、担保条項の比較

paragraphs.text:
Exclusions/ Exceptions Conditions Warranties
▪ They are used to define and restrict the boundaries of the policy coverage.
▪ They allow repudiation if the circumstances of a claim indicate that the exclusion applies.
▪ They are always specified in the policy, and usually require the insurer to prove that the exclusion applies.
▪ Implied conditions must be complied with, otherwise there is doubt as to the validity of the entire contract.
▪ Conditions precedent to the contract may either be concerned with the formation of contract or be ongoing.
▪ Conditions subsequent to the contract and/or precedent to liability give the insurers the right to repudiate a claim, but not to repudiate the contract in its entirety.
▪ They must be strictly and literally complied with.
▪ They give the insurer the right to repudiate on any breach, even if the breach did not result in the loss or damage.
▪ They are written into the policy, except where implied.
paragraphs.textJP:
免責事項 | 条件 | 担保条項
▪ 保険カバーの境界を定義し制限するために使用されます。
▪ 保険金請求の状況が免責事項に該当することが示された場合、保険金請求の拒否を可能にします。
▪ 常に保険証券に記載されており、通常、保険者が適用を証明する必要があります。

paragraphs.text: The wording of this clause which authenticates the policy document depends on each insurer’s procedure on the signing of the policy document. It may read as simply as “In witness whereof, this Policy has been signed by and on behalf of the Company”. This is usually signed by an authorised officer of the insurance company, and this binds the insurer to the insurance contract. The date is not stated as it appears in the schedule.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券を認証するこの条項の文言は、保険証券の署名に関する各保険者の手続きによって異なります。単に「その証として、本保険証券は会社を代表して署名されました」と記載される場合があります。これは通常、保険会社の権限を有する役員によって署名されます。

paragraphs.text: Since everyone’s circumstances are different, people often wish to make a change to a standard version of a policy. As things change, existing policies sometimes need to be modified. For example, an additional insured may need to be included in a policy, or additional protection may be needed for certain property. The insurer can effect any such change by passing (adding) an endorsement to the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: すべての人の状況は異なるため、人々はしばしば保険証券の標準版に変更を加えることを望みます。状況が変わるにつれ、既存の保険証券を修正する必要が生じることがあります。例えば、追加の被保険者を保険証券に含める必要がある場合や、特定のリスクに対して追加の保護が必要な場合があります。

paragraphs.text: The policy, together with the schedule and any endorsement, forms the evidence of the insurance contract. Any endorsement must be read together with the policy, and any word or expression to which a specific meaning has been attached bears such meaning whenever it appears. The effective date (of the endorsement or changes) and the wording of the endorsement effecting the change are of significant importance.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券は、スケジュールおよび裏書とともに、保険契約の証拠を構成します。裏書は保険証券と併せて読まなければならず、特定の意味が付与された語句は、出現するたびにその意味を有します。裏書の発効日は、裏書に記載されます。

裏書が必要となりうる状況の例は以下のとおりです。
▪ 保険金額の変更
▪ 利害関係者または被保険利益の変更
▪ 物理的リスクの変更（例：所在地、職業）
▪ 住所の変更
▪ 保険期間の延長または変更
▪ 保険の解約
▪ リスクの削除

paragraphs.text: Usually, a month or so, before a policy expires, the insured receives a renewal notice, also known as an expiry notice, reminding the insured that the policy is due to expire, and the existing terms are usually specified.
paragraphs.textJP: 通常、保険証券の満了の約1ヶ月前に、被保険者は更新通知（満了通知とも呼ばれます）を受け取り、保険証券が満了予定であること、および既存の条件が通常記載されています。

paragraphs.text: Where the insurer is going to change the terms on renewal, the intentions are clearly stated in the notice, and the offer of renewal by the insurer is clearly stated in it.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者が更新時に条件を変更する場合、その意図は通知に明確に記載され、保険者による更新の申出が明確に記載されます。

paragraphs.text: At times, owing to adverse claims experience, the insurer may decline renewal of the policy, and such intention is either stamped or typed on the expiry notice.
paragraphs.textJP: 時には、不利な保険金請求実績のために、保険者は保険証券の更新を拒否することがあり、そのような意図は満了通知にスタンプまたはタイプされます。

paragraphs.text: It should be noted that, upon receipt of the renewal notice, the insured may of course change the sum insured, etc. However, at all times, the implied condition of utmost good faith applies, and the insured is duty bound to disclose all material facts existing at the time of the renewal.
paragraphs.textJP: 更新通知を受け取った際、被保険者は保険金額等を変更できることに留意すべきです。ただし、常に最大善意の黙示的条件が適用され、被保険者は更新時に存在するすべての重要事実を告知する義務を負います。

通知に含まれる情報は、通常、元の保険証券およびすべての裏書に含まれるのと同じ情報です。加えて、被保険者が変更を要求するためのセクションがあります。情報には以下が含まれます。
▪ 被保険者の氏名と住所
▪ 保険証券番号
▪ 満了日または保険期間

paragraphs.text: A renewal notice is not a legal requirement, and the insurer has the option whether or not to send it. However, business efficiency normally dictates that it should be sent to the insured.
paragraphs.textJP: 更新通知は法的義務ではなく、保険者にはそれを送付するかどうかの選択肢があります。しかし、業務効率上、通常は被保険者に送付されるべきです。

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, the Singapore General Insurance Code of Practice issued by GIA states that the insurer will inform the insured when he needs to renew his policy or when his policy will end, at least 30 business days before expiry of the policy, to allow him to consider and arrange for continuing cover. However, the Singapore General Insurance Code of Practice applies only to personal lines of general insurance.
paragraphs.textJP: さらに、GIAが発行したシンガポール損害保険行動規範は、保険者が被保険者に対し、保険証券の満了の少なくとも30営業日前に、保険証券の更新が必要であること、または保険証券が終了することを通知し、継続的なカバーの手配を検討する時間を確保すべきであると定めています。

paragraphs.text: The insurer may issue a renewal certificate once the insured confirms that he is renewing the policy on the same terms, or with minor changes, such as the sum insured.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者は、被保険者が同じ条件で、または保険金額等の軽微な変更を伴って保険証券を更新することを確認した後、更新証書を発行することがあります。

更新証書は保険証券のスケジュールに類似しており、保険証券に対する変更（もしあれば）を一覧にします。相互参照および識別のために以下の情報を含みます。
▪ 被保険者の氏名と住所
▪ 保険証券番号
▪ 保険期間
▪ 代理店／口座番号

paragraphs.text: It is normal for an insurer to receive a phone call or an e-mail concerning an event leading to a claim almost immediately after the accident.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者が、事故の直後に保険金請求につながる事象に関する電話やメールを受けることは一般的です。

paragraphs.text: Following that, it is essential that the insurer’s claim form be completed and submitted for processing and settling of a loss under the policy. At the same time, the insurer must have precise information on the circumstances and extent of the loss and must determine if the loss falls within the policy coverage. The claim form collects pertinent information on the accident, injury, illness, loss or damage suffered by the insured.
paragraphs.textJP: その後、保険証券に基づく損失の処理および決済のために、保険者の保険金請求書を記入し提出することが不可欠です。同時に、保険者は損失の状況および範囲に関する正確な情報を把握し、損失が保険証券の範囲内にあるかどうかを判断しなければなりません。

paragraphs.text: Different claim forms are used among insurers, and different claim forms are used for different classes of insurance. They are usually available for downloading from the websites of the insurers.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者によって異なる保険金請求書が使用され、保険の種類によっても異なる保険金請求書が使用されます。これらは通常、保険者のウェブサイトからダウンロードできます。

paragraphs.text: The insurer usually states on the claim form that it is issued without any admission of liability, or without prejudice.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者は通常、保険金請求書に、いかなる賠償責任の承認もなく、または権利留保で発行される旨を記載します。

paragraphs.text: The claim form is broadly divided into the following sections:
paragraphs.textJP: 保険金請求書は、大まかに以下のセクションに分かれています。

subsections.label: I
subsections.title: Policyholder’s/Claimant’s Particulars
subsections.titleJP: I. 保険契約者／請求者の詳細

paragraphs.text: a) Name, address and contact details b) Policy particulars
paragraphs.textJP:
(a) 氏名、住所、連絡先
(b) 保険証券の詳細

paragraphs.text: a) Circumstances of loss b) Date of loss c) Place of loss d) Any third parties involved e) Details of any witnesses III. Details Of Injuries/Loss/Damage/Suffered a) Description of property loss or damage b) Details of injuries sustained
paragraphs.textJP:
(a) 損失の状況
(b) 損失の日付
(c) 損失の場所
(d) 関与する第三者
(e) 目撃者の詳細
III. 傷害／損失／損害の詳細
(a) 財物の損失または損害の記述
(b) 受傷の詳細

paragraphs.text: a) Police Report b) Medical Report c) Statements made (if any) to and/or by third parties, etc.
paragraphs.textJP:
(a) 警察報告書
(b) 医療報告書
(c) 第三者に対してなされた、および／または第三者による供述（もしあれば）

subsections.label: V
subsections.title: Insured’s Declaration, Signature & Date
subsections.titleJP: V. 被保険者の宣言・署名・日付

paragraphs.text: NOTE: Since there are wide variations in the claim forms of various insurance companies, readers should do a compare-and-contrast study of various forms from different insurance companies.
paragraphs.textJP: 注意：各保険会社の保険金請求書には大きな差異があるため、読者は異なる保険会社のさまざまな書式を比較・対照して研究すべきです。

paragraphs.text: Details of the handling and settlement of claims will be discussed in the next chapter of this Study Text. ABC INSURANCE COMPANY LIMITED (Designated Insurer) (A member of the General Insurance Association of Singapore) Chief Executive AN EXTRACT FROM THE WORK INJURY COMPENSATION (INSURANCE) REGULATIONS 2020 5 (1) Where a certificate of insurance has been issued to an employer in accordance with regulation 4, the employer must display a copy of the certificate of insurance in accordance with paragraphs (2) and (3) throughout the period of validity of the insurance policy to which the certificate relates - (a) at the employer’s place of business; or (b) where the employer has more than one place of business, at each place of business at which the employer employs any employee whose claims may be the subject of an indemnity under the insurance policy to which that certificate relates. (2) The copies of certificates of insurance displayed for the purposes of paragraph (1) must be displayed in a manner that is easily seen and read by every employee whose claims may be the subject of indemnity under the insurance policy to which the certificate relates. (3) Copies of certificates of insurance must be displayed at the places mentioned in paragraph (1)(a) or (b) only during the period of validity of the insurance policy to which the certificate relates. (4) In this regulation, the period of validity of an insurance policy is the period - (a) starting on the date of commencement of the insurance policy; and (b) ending on the date of expiry or cancellation of that insurance policy. (5) A person who contravenes paragraph (1) shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on conviction - (a) to a fine not exceeding $2,000; or (b) if the person is a repeat offender, to a fine not exceeding $4,000. Appendix 6E ABC INSURANCE COMPANY (SINGAPORE) LIMITED (Registration No: 2009-12345-A) 21 Any Street, ABC Centre, Singapore 654321 Tel: (65) 6789 8181 Fax: (65) 6789 8282 E-mail: contactus@abcinco.com.sg Website: www.abcinco.com.sg PERSONAL ACCIDENT INSURANCE POLICY DEFINITION OF WORDS Certain words have been defined below. These have the same meaning wherever they are used in the Policy or the Schedule. They appear in bold print (e.g. Insured Person, Injury) or begin with a capital letter (e.g. You, We). We, Us, Our, the Company ABC Insurance Company (Singapore) Limited You, Your, the Insured The policy owner named as Insured in the Schedule. Insured Person Each of the persons named as such in the Schedule who meets the eligibility criteria set out in General Condition 3 of the Policy. Schedule The Schedule containing details of the Insured Person(s), type of cover selected and Period of Insurance. The Schedule forms part of the Policy. Period of Insurance The period shown in the Schedule, and any further period for which You agree to pay and We agree to accept premium. Commencement Date Original inception date of cover under this Policy as shown in the Schedule. Child A person named as the Insured Person in the Schedule who is the Insured's biological or legally adopted and unmarried Child aged between one year to 18 years or 23 years if pursuing full-time education in a recognised tertiary institution within his/her Usual Country of Residence as Singapore. Accident An event which happens suddenly and gives rise to a result which the Insured Person did not intend or anticipate. Injury Bodily injury suffered anywhere in the world caused solely by Accident and not by sickness, disease or gradual physical or mental wear and tear. Physician A qualified medical practitioner other than You, Your relative, the Insured Person or the Insured Person's relative who is licensed by the Medical Authorities of the country in which treatment is provided to practise Western medicine and surgery, and who in rendering such treatment is practicing within the scope of his or her licensing and training in his/her geographical area of practice. SPECIMEN Hospital A lawfully operating institution for the care and treatment of sick and injured persons, which has 24 hours nursing services by registered graduate nurses, one or more Physicians available at all times and organised facilities for diagnosis and major surgery, which shall not primarily be a clinic, a place for alcoholics or drug addicts, a nursing, rest or convalescent home, home for the aged or similar establishment. Chinese Physician A person other than You, Your relative, the Insured Person or the Insured Person's relative engaging in the practice of traditional Chinese medicine and/or acupuncture (including a herbalist, bonesetter or chiropractor), who is duly licensed or registered to practise according to the laws and regulations applicable in the geographical area of his/her practice. Daily Hospitalisation Cash Benefit Cash benefit payable at an agreed sum per day in the event of an Insured Person's confinement in a Hospital for a continuous uninterrupted period of at least 24 hours upon the advice of a Physician for which the Hospital makes a charge for room and board. Usual Country of Residence The country in which the Insured Person is usually living at the inception of this insurance. As a condition precedent to liability, the Company must be informed in writing of any change in the Usual Country of Residence. A permanent change in the Usual Country of Residence is deemed to occur when the Insured Person lives or intends to live in another country for more than 6 consecutive months during any Period of Insurance. The Company reserves the right to continue cover on the terms and conditions it considers appropriate to the new country of residence or to decline to continue cover under the Policy. Burns 3rd degree burns. Weekly Benefit Compensation payable at a rate per week. THE BENEFITS We will pay You: The Compensation for death or disablement (the Results) as described below if the Insured Person suffers Injury which within 104 weeks of its happening is the sale cause of the death or such disablement.
